examples: If you are walking along heathland, try to tread heavily to warn away snakes.
Holding steady: the wood or heathland ant holding a microchip in its toothed (serrated) mandibles.
With so much land to work with, Jones wove together a tapestry of heathland, hardwoods, streams, and wetlands.
There were 150 hectares of heathland on fire with a fire front of about a mile-and-a-half.
The proposed development, at Crete's Cavo Sidero peninsula, would include accommodation and a heathland golf course.
